# Consistency across outcomes {#sec-joint}

::: {.callout-note}
Drafted by a LLM-based AI tool (Claude Code/Opus 4.8). Scaffold — an outline of intended content and its source artifacts, not finished prose.
:::

::: {.callout-important title="Causal status — [CAUSAL]"}
The joint model estimates the same randomised $\tau$ for every outcome at once; all contrasts below are between **causal** effects.
:::

*[Scaffold]* One posterior over the suite, enabling cross-outcome comparison. *Source:* the joint model (LRPITT12), `tau_forest.png`, ITT-vs-joint consistency CSVs.

## The joint forest {#sec-joint-forest}

*[Scaffold]* $\tau_k$ for each outcome on one figure; confirm the joint $\tau$ reproduce the single-outcome $\tau$ (@sec-itt).

## Cross-outcome contrasts {#sec-joint-contrasts}

*[Scaffold]* $P(\tau_k > \tau_j)$ — e.g. is the effect on one skill more positive than on another?

## Generalisation: taught vs not-taught {#sec-joint-generalisation}

*[Scaffold]* The taught-minus-untaught contrast (LRPITT15/15b): directly-taught words move more than comparison words — limited generalisation. *Source:* `tau_difference.csv`.
